tool_args.label: MTA ridership vs employment trend comparison
tool_args.raw_text: From January 2011 through February 2020, the twelve-month employment average increased 24.1%, while the 12-month MTA-wide ridership average fell 0.7%. The COVID pandemic has altered these trajectories: compared with January 2017, July employment is 5.8% higher, while MTA-wide ridership is 38.3% lower.

payload.claim_text: From January 2011 through February 2020, the twelve-month employment average increased 24.1%, while the 12-month MTA-wide ridership average fell 0.7%. The COVID pandemic has altered these trajectories: compared with January 2017, July employment is 5.8% higher, while MTA-wide ridership is 38.3% lower.

tool_args.label: NYC employment recovery 28th consecutive month
tool_args.raw_text: Preliminary July 2023 employment in New York City marks the 28th consecutive month of employment recovery, compared with one year earlier, since the COVID pandemic began, with an increase of 152,100 jobs (3.4%) when compared with July 2022.

payload.claim_text: Preliminary July 2023 employment in New York City marks the 28th consecutive month of employment recovery, with an increase of 152,100 jobs (3.4%) when compared with July 2022.
